summary: Excellent audience recording captures a high‑energy 1984 set highlighted by a blistering Scarlet > Touch > Fire and scorching Alabama > Promised Land.
review: The tape offers crystal‑clear, airy sound that lets every instrument shine, especially Brent’s synth work and Jerry’s searing guitar solos.  The show is praised for its electric first set and a pre‑drums Scarlet > Touch > Fire that many reviewers call one of the best ever, with Deal, Throwing Stones and Don’t Need Love also standing out.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5), strong praise for both performance and audio quality, with many reviewers calling it a standout 84 show.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, airy 3‑D soundfield; well‑balanced mix with Brent on the right channel and prominent Phil bass. Minor ambient crowd noise but overall pristine and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set builds into a tight, tension‑filled pre‑drums segment; Scarlet > Touch > Fire serves as the high point before a spirited second half with strong finishes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for scorching solos on Scarlet, Fire, Deal and Alabama; described as shredding, fuzzed out and locked in deep with Phil.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for dropping bombs on West L.A., providing a solid bass foundation throughout, especially during the tight pre‑drums.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for strong vocals and guitar on Esau, a memorable Bobby rap on Lost (LLR) and humorous moments on Throwing Stones.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboards called emotional, trippy and central to the Scarlet > Touch > Fire transition, adding depth to the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums segment described as tight and energetic; Bill’s playing drives the Scarlet > Touch > Fire sequence.
